original_poem:
मी झाड बोलतोय  
आसमानाच्या छायेत,  
पानांच्या आभाळात  
संपूर्ण जगणं अनुभवतोय,  
मी रुखमाई फुलते.
translated_poem:
I am a talking tree  
In the shade of the sky,  
In the canopy of leaves  
I experience all of life,  
As the flowers bloom.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

translated_lines: In the shade of the sky,

emotional_arc.value: A tree speaks with quiet authority, moving from sheltered existence under sky and leaf canopy to a full embrace of life, culminating in a flowering self-identification with Rukhmabai.

translated_lines: In the shade of the sky,

loss_note.value: रुखमाई is rendered as generic 'flowers bloom', erasing the specific deity name and its devotional resonance.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: the tree as a living witness voicing its own rooted existence
the leaf canopy as a self-contained sky sheltering all of life's experience
the speaker's identity blossoming into the divine feminine of Rukhmabai
tree-life holds the fullness of existence

visual_motifs.motif: a dense canopy of leaves like a sky overhead
a flowering tree identified with रुखमाई
a speaking tree standing beneath an open sky
a speaking tree under the sky's shade
dense green leaf canopy spread like a second sky overhead
flowers blooming on a tree in the image of Rukhmabai

translation_note.value: Rukhmabai, the consort of Vitthal venerated in Varkari tradition, is replaced by the generic phrase 'flowers bloom'.
